title: NGO Office Migration: Coordinating Large-Scale Moves for International Non-Profits in Old Airport
excerpt: NGO office migration Old Airport: international non-profit relocation, donor audit trails, field office equipment, large-scale coordination, Addis NGO belt movers.
content:
NGO office migration: coordinating large-scale moves for international non-profits in Old Airport

Old Airport concentrates NGO headquarters—donor visibility, expat staff housing nearby, and offices that accumulate field equipment, grant archives, and fleet paperwork. Large-scale NGO migration is program continuity exercise: move must not delay proposal deadline, lose audit trail, or strand generator bound for Somali region in wrong warehouse bay.

This guide coordinates NGO office migrations in the Old Airport belt.

Stakeholder map

  • Country director: Sign-off on budget and downtime.
  • Finance: Asset register and donor tagging.
  • IT: Server, VPN, field laptop imaging.
  • Programs: Field kit priority list.
  • HR: Staff desk and personnel file custody.
  • Landlord old and new: Handover and deposit.

Asset register alignment

Every tagged item matches inventory before truck—donor audit depends on it. Split donor-funded equipment in manifest. Disposal of obsolete gear documented per grant rules—not silent dumpster.

Phased migration waves

Wave 1 — archive and low-use

Historical files, seasonal field gear to storage.

Wave 2 — programs and IT

Servers, program team, meeting rooms.

Wave 3 — reception and executive

Client-facing ready Monday; signage updated.

Field operations continuity

Warehouse zone for emergency health kits ships before general FF&E if outbreak response active—programs lead overrides default load order.

Security and data

Beneficiary data servers encrypted transport; personnel files locked cartons. Visitor log at both sites during transition week.

Old Airport access

Compound gates familiar with NGO trucks—still book slot. Residential-adjacent offices: noise and hour limits. Expat housing overflow parking for crew—confirm.

Vendor procurement

Three-quote policy many NGOs—compare line items not only total. Insurance and child-safeguarding policy alignment for vendor on premises.

Active Movers NGO references

Large-scale NGO moves with asset tagging support—disclose headcount and field warehouse at RFP.

Conclusion

NGO Old Airport migrations coordinate stakeholders, asset registers, phased waves, and field priority—large-scale move succeeds when programs still deploy Monday, not when last carton leaves.
author: Active Movers Team
